meta.title: India States Job Market 2023-24
meta.primarySource: PLFS Annual Report 2023-24, MoSPI + State Economic Surveys 2023-24
meta.notes: State workforces estimated using PLFS 2023-24 state-wise WPR × projected state populations (Census 2011 base). Industry breakdowns use PLFS state tables, NASSCOM state data, state Economic Surveys, and ASI 2022-23. Salaries reflect PLFS 2023-24 state-wise average monthly earnings for regular salaried workers.

sectors.name: Uttar Pradesh
sources: UP Economic Survey 2023-24, GoUP Finance Dept.

occupations.name: Agriculture & Farming
occupations.description: India's largest agricultural state. Wheat, sugarcane, potato, rice. 45M agri workers; still employs 51% of UP workforce.
sources: Directorate of Agriculture & Food Production, GoUP

occupations.name: MSME, Handlooms & Crafts
occupations.description: Varanasi silk, Agra leather, Moradabad brassware, Kanpur textiles. UP has India's largest MSME cluster base.
sources: MSME Annual Report 2023-24 — UP MSME count

occupations.name: Construction & Infrastructure
occupations.description: Expressway projects (Purvanchal, Bundelkhand), PM Awas Yojana housing, Jewar Airport driving construction boom.

occupations.name: Trade & Commerce
occupations.description: Retail trade, wholesale markets across 75 districts. Agra, Kanpur, Varanasi are major commercial centres.

occupations.name: IT & New Economy (Noida/Lucknow)
occupations.description: Noida (NCR) hosts 1,000+ IT firms. Lucknow Tech City emerging. UP IT exports ₹30,000 Cr FY24.
sources: NASSCOM State IT Report 2025 — UP IT workforce

occupations.name: Government & Public Services
occupations.description: UP has India's largest state government workforce. UPPSC, UPSSSC recruitments employ lakhs across 75 districts.

sectors.name: Maharashtra
sources: Maharashtra Economic Survey 2023-24, Finance Dept. GoM

occupations.name: Finance, IT & Professional Services
occupations.description: Mumbai = India's financial capital. Pune = 2nd largest IT hub. Maharashtra IT exports ₹2.4L Cr FY24; 14L IT employees.
sources: NASSCOM State IT Report 2025 — Maharashtra 1.4M IT workers
RBI Annual Report 2024 — Mumbai financial sector employment

occupations.name: Manufacturing & Engineering
occupations.description: Pune auto hub (Tata, Bajaj, Mercedes), Nashik (Mahindra, Sula wines), Nagpur SEZ, Aurangabad AURIC. Maharashtra = 15% of India's industrial output.

occupations.name: Agriculture (Sugarcane, Cotton)
occupations.description: Maharashtra is largest sugarcane and cotton producer. Vidarbha farmer distress; drip irrigation adoption growing.
sources: Maharashtra Dept. of Agriculture Annual Report 2023-24

occupations.name: Trade, Hospitality & Tourism
occupations.description: Mumbai retail, hospitality, Ajanta-Ellora tourism. Maharashtra received 12Cr domestic tourists FY24.

occupations.name: Construction & Real Estate
occupations.description: Mumbai Metro 2A/7, Pune Ring Road, Nagpur-Mumbai Samruddhi Expressway. Maharashtra accounts for 25% of India's real estate market.

occupations.name: Government & Public Sector
occupations.description: State government, BEST, MSEDCL, MMRDA, PSU employees. Maharashtra 7th Pay Commission equivalent scales.
sources: Maharashtra Finance Dept. Budget 2024-25 — staff expenditure

sectors.name: Bihar
sources: Bihar Economic Survey 2023-24, Finance Dept. GoB

occupations.name: Agriculture & Allied
occupations.description: Bihar = major rice, wheat, maize, litchi, makhana producer. ~55% workforce in agriculture. Heavy flood-dependence.

occupations.name: Construction & Infrastructure
occupations.description: Bihar Road Works, Ganga Bridge projects, rural housing. Construction is fastest-growing formal employer in Bihar.

occupations.name: Trade & Migrant Remittance Economy
occupations.description: Largest source of migrant labour to Delhi, Punjab, Maharashtra, Gujarat. Remittances ₹35,000 Cr/yr support rural consumption.
sources: Economic Survey of India 2023-24 Bihar migration data

occupations.name: Government & Education Services
occupations.description: State government, Jeevika SHG network, BPSC recruits. Bihar's education hiring (Niyojit teachers) is among India's largest.

occupations.name: MSME & Food Processing
occupations.description: Muzaffarpur litchi processing, Bhagalpur silk (Tussar), Madhubani handlooms, makhana processing SMEs.
sources: One District One Product (ODOP) Bihar data 2024

sectors.name: West Bengal
sources: West Bengal Economic Review 2023-24, Finance Dept. GoWB

occupations.name: Agriculture (Rice, Jute, Tea)
occupations.description: India's largest rice and jute producer. Darjeeling/Dooars tea plantations employ 1M. West Bengal: 38% workforce in agriculture.
sources: Tea Board of India Annual Report 2023-24 — 1M tea workers

occupations.name: Manufacturing & Engineering
occupations.description: Haldia Petrochemicals, Durgapur steel, Howrah engineering cluster. WB historically strong in jute mills (declining) and metal goods.
sources: WBIDC (WB Industrial Development Corp.) Annual Report 2024

occupations.name: IT & Knowledge Services (Kolkata)
occupations.description: Sector V Salt Lake (IT hub), Newtown Kolkata FinTech hub. WB IT exports ₹42,000 Cr FY24; growing faster than national average.
sources: NASSCOM State IT Report 2025 — WB IT workforce 5L

occupations.name: Trade, Informal & Gig Economy
occupations.description: Kolkata wholesale market, Burrabazar, border trade with Bangladesh/Nepal. Large informal hawker economy in Kolkata.

occupations.name: Government & PSUs
occupations.description: State govt, WBSEDCL, Kolkata Metro Railway, PSU headquarters (Coal India, SAIL, Exide). WB has high public sector density.
sources: Coal India HQ Kolkata employee data 2024

occupations.name: Artisans, Weavers & Creative
occupations.description: Baluchari, Jamdani, Kantha handlooms; Bishnupur terracotta; Kumartuli potters. WB's handloom sector employs 7L weavers.
sources: WB Handloom & Textile Dept. Annual Report 2023-24

sectors.name: Tamil Nadu
sources: Tamil Nadu Economic Appraisal 2023-24, Finance Dept. GoTN

occupations.name: Automobile & Auto Components
occupations.description: Chennai = 'Detroit of India'. Ford, Hyundai, BMW, Renault-Nissan, TVS, Ashok Leyland. TN produces 35% of India's vehicles.

occupations.name: Textile, Garment & Leather
occupations.description: Tiruppur knitwear (₹35,000 Cr exports), Chennai leather (40% of India's leather exports), Coimbatore spinning mills.
sources: AEPC (Apparel Export Promotion Council) TN data 2024
CLE (Council for Leather Exports) Annual Report 2023-24

occupations.name: IT & BPO Services (Chennai)
occupations.description: Chennai is India's 3rd largest IT city. 20L IT workers; Cognizant, Infosys, TCS, Zoho HQ. TN IT exports ₹1.5L Cr FY24.
sources: NASSCOM State IT Report 2025 — TN 2M IT workers
STPI Chennai Annual Performance Report FY24

occupations.name: Agriculture & Fisheries
occupations.description: Rice, bananas, turmeric, groundnut. Tamil Nadu is India's leading seafood exporter (₹12,000 Cr FY24).
sources: MPEDA (Marine Products Export Development Authority) TN data 2024

occupations.name: Healthcare & Higher Education Hub
occupations.description: Chennai = India's healthcare hub (medical tourism ₹8,000 Cr). TN leads in MBBS seats; Apollo, Fortis, MIOT anchor a growing export-quality healthcare sector.

occupations.name: Trade, Services & Government
occupations.description: Retail trade across 37 districts, TNPSC government jobs, TANGEDCO, TIDCO public sector. TN per-capita income ₹2.5L, well above national average.

sectors.name: Karnataka
sources: Karnataka Economic Survey 2023-24, Finance Dept. GoK

occupations.name: IT, Startups & Digital Economy (Bengaluru)
occupations.description: Bengaluru = India's Silicon Valley. 30L IT workers, 60K+ startups, 45 unicorns. Karnataka IT exports $50Bn FY24 (38% of India's total).
sources: NASSCOM State IT Report 2025 — Karnataka 3M IT workers

occupations.name: Aerospace, Defence & Electronics
occupations.description: HAL, ISRO, DRDO, BEL, NAL HQ in Bengaluru. Karnataka accounts for 65% of India's aerospace output.

occupations.name: Agriculture, Coffee & Horticulture
occupations.description: Karnataka produces 70% of India's coffee, 60% of silk (Mysuru), largest ragi producer. Coorg coffee estates employ 3L workers.
sources: Coffee Board of India Annual Report 2023-24 — Karnataka 70% production

occupations.name: Manufacturing & Engineering
occupations.description: Mysuru machinery, Dharwad textiles, Hubli-Dharwad industrial estates, Tumkur electronics PLI units.
sources: KSSIDC (Karnataka Small Scale Industries Development Corp.) Annual Report 2024

occupations.name: Trade, Hospitality & Tourism
occupations.description: Bengaluru retail (India's highest per-capita consumption city), Mysuru-Coorg tourism (₹15,000 Cr). Karnataka received 17Cr domestic tourists FY24.

occupations.name: Government & Public Services
occupations.description: State government, BESCOM, KSRTC, BMRCL, Sarakari Shakti recruitment. KPSC among India's most competitive state PSCs.
sources: GoK Finance Dept. Budget 2024-25 — salary expenditure

sectors.name: Gujarat
sources: Gujarat Economic Survey 2023-24, Finance Dept. GoG

occupations.name: Chemicals, Pharma & Petrochemicals
occupations.description: Gujarat produces 35% of India's chemicals and 40% of pharma exports. Ankleshwar, Vapi, Dahej chemical clusters; Ahmedabad-Vadodara pharma belt.
sources: Indian Chemical Council Gujarat Chapter Report 2024

occupations.name: Diamond, Textile & Apparel
occupations.description: Surat processes 90% of world's cut-and-polished diamonds; 8L diamond workers. Surat also = synthetic textile capital of India.
sources: GJEPC (Gems & Jewellery Export Promotion Council) Surat data 2024 — 90% global diamond processing
Southern Gujarat Chamber of Commerce Textile Report 2024

occupations.name: Ports, Logistics & Trade
occupations.description: Gujarat handles 40% of India's maritime cargo via Mundra, Kandla, Hazira ports. GIFT City (India's first IFSC) adds FinTech jobs.
sources: Sagarmala Programme Gujarat Ports data FY24
IFSCA (International Financial Services Centres Authority) GIFT City Report 2024

occupations.name: Agriculture & Dairy (Amul Belt)
occupations.description: GCMMF (Amul) cooperative model covers 36L dairy farmers. Gujarat leads in cotton, groundnut, castor oil. White Revolution model.

occupations.name: Heavy Engineering & Auto
occupations.description: Tata Nano plant (now EV), Maruti Suzuki Hansalpur, Ford Sanand (repurposed for Tata EV), Adani Wilmar, ONGC Hazira.
sources: GIDC (Gujarat Industrial Development Corp.) Annual Report 2023-24

occupations.name: Renewable Energy & New Sectors
occupations.description: Rewa Ultra Mega Solar, Adani Green, Torrent Power, RE-Invest 2024 pledges. Gujarat targets 100GW RE by 2030.
sources: GEDA (Gujarat Energy Development Agency) Annual Report 2023-24
MNRE Gujarat Solar/Wind installed capacity data 2024

sectors.name: Rajasthan
sources: Rajasthan Economic Review 2023-24, Finance Dept. GoR

occupations.name: Agriculture & Animal Husbandry
occupations.description: Rajasthan = largest wheat & mustard producer; largest livestock population in India. Arid farming; Indira Gandhi Canal region high-yield wheat.
sources: DAHD Animal Husbandry census 2023 — Rajasthan largest cattle state

occupations.name: Mining & Marble (Rajasthan = India's Mineral State)
occupations.description: Rajasthan has India's richest mineral base: zinc (Hindustan Zinc, Udaipur), marble (Makrana), limestone, gypsum, sand stone. 23% of national mineral production.
sources: Ministry of Mines Annual Report 2023-24 — Rajasthan 23% output
IBM (Indian Bureau of Mines) Rajasthan Mineral Data 2024

occupations.name: Tourism, Handicrafts & Gems
occupations.description: Jaipur (Pink City) Blue Pottery, Jodhpur furniture, Jaisalmer tourism. Rajasthan received 15Cr domestic tourists FY24; Jaipur = top gems & jewellery hub.
sources: Rajasthan Tourism Dept. Annual Report 2023-24 — 15Cr tourists

occupations.name: Construction & Infrastructure
occupations.description: Delhi-Mumbai Industrial Corridor (DMIC) nodes at Neemrana, Khushkhera. PM Awas Yojana housing. Expressway projects: Delhi-Vadodara-Mumbai.
sources: DMIC Project Implementation Trust Fund — Rajasthan node progress 2024
RUIFDCO (Rajasthan Urban Infra Finance Dev Corp.) 2024

occupations.name: Renewable Energy (Solar Capital)
occupations.description: Rajasthan = India's #1 solar state: 25GW+ installed, Bhadla Solar Park (2,245 MW, world's largest). Targets 90GW by 2030.
sources: Rajasthan Renewable Energy Corporation (RRECL) Annual Report 2023-24

occupations.name: Trade, Government & Services
occupations.description: RPSC government services, Jaipur retail, Ajmer textile trade. Rajasthan per-capita GSDP growing at 11% CAGR.

occupations.name: Agriculture & Aquaculture
occupations.description: AP is India's largest aquaculture state (shrimp exports $3Bn, 40% of national total). Also major rice, tobacco, chilli producer.
sources: MPEDA AP aquaculture data 2023-24 — 40% of India's shrimp exports

occupations.name: IT & Knowledge Economy (Vizag, Amaravati)
occupations.description: Vizag Pharma City SEZ, Amaravati IT corridor, AP Innovation Society. AP IT exports growing rapidly post-capital construction restart.
sources: AP Economic Development Board IT data 2024

occupations.name: Pharma, Ports & Manufacturing
occupations.description: Vizag Pharma City (bulk drugs), Krishnapatnam & Gangavaram ports, Nellore MSME. AP = 2nd in pharma production by value.

occupations.name: Trade, Tourism & Services
occupations.description: Tirupati temple tourism (1.5Cr visitors/year), Vijayawada retail, Vizag port city services.

occupations.name: Government & Social Welfare
occupations.description: APPSC, Rythu Bharosa scheme employees, AP Grama Volunteers (2.7L). AP government a major direct employer.

sectors.name: Telangana
sources: Telangana Economic Outlook 2023-24, Finance Dept. GoTS

occupations.name: IT, Pharma & Life Sciences (Hyderabad)
occupations.description: Hyderabad = India's HITEC City (3rd largest IT hub). 10L IT workers. Genome Valley = Asia's largest pharma cluster. Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Apple major presence.
sources: NASSCOM State IT Report 2025 — TS 1M+ IT workers

occupations.name: Agriculture & Horticulture
occupations.description: Rytu Bandhu scheme supports 69L farmers. Major paddy, maize, cotton, turmeric producer. Drip irrigation adoption leading nationally.

occupations.name: Manufacturing, Aerospace & Defence
occupations.description: Hyderabad = 2nd aerospace hub after Bengaluru (BHEL, DRDO, BDL). Pharma manufacturing, Warangal textiles, Zaheerabad fertilisers.
sources: TSIIC (TS Industrial Infrastructure Corp.) Annual Report 2024

occupations.name: Trade, Real Estate & Hospitality
occupations.description: Hyderabad realty among India's hottest markets. Old City pearl/bangle trade. Telangana MICE tourism growing.
sources: Knight Frank Hyderabad Real Estate Report H2 2024

occupations.name: Government & Public Services
occupations.description: TSPSC, Central government offices (ECIL, BDL, BSNL), TS Transco/DISCOMS. Hyderabad = Central/State government office hub.

sectors.name: Madhya Pradesh
sources: Madhya Pradesh Economic Survey 2023-24, Finance Dept. GoMP

occupations.name: Agriculture (Soybean, Wheat, Pulses)
occupations.description: MP = largest soybean, chickpea, lentil, garlic producer. Wheat output growing rapidly post-MSP hike. Agriculture 48% of workforce.

occupations.name: Mining (Diamonds, Coal, Manganese)
occupations.description: Panna = India's only diamond mining site. Singrauli coalfields, Balaghat copper, Jabalpur cement. MP ranks 5th in mineral production.
sources: IBM Mineral Production Statistics 2024 — MP 5th rank

occupations.name: Tourism, Temples & Handicrafts
occupations.description: Khajuraho temples, Kanha-Bandhavgarh tigers, Ujjain Mahakaleshwar. MP received 9.5Cr domestic tourists FY24 — 3rd highest nationally.
sources: MP Tourism Dept. Annual Report 2023-24 — 9.5Cr tourists

occupations.name: Construction & Infrastructure
occupations.description: Delhi-Mumbai Expressway, Narmada Valley Development, Smart Cities (Bhopal, Indore). MP GSDP growing at 13% CAGR 2018-24.

occupations.name: Trade, Services & IT (Indore)
occupations.description: Indore = cleanest city 7 years running, emerging IT hub. Pithampur auto-ancillary zone. MP IT exports ₹12,000 Cr FY24.

sectors.name: Kerala
sources: Kerala Economic Review 2023-24, State Planning Board, GoK

occupations.name: NRI Remittance & Gulf Economy
occupations.description: 35L Keralites abroad (mostly Gulf); remittances ₹1.15L Cr FY24 = 30% of Kerala GSDP. Highest literacy + social indicators driven by Gulf money.
sources: Norka Roots (NRI Affairs Dept. Kerala) Annual Report 2023-24 — ₹1.15L Cr remittance
Kerala State Planning Board Economic Review 2023-24

occupations.name: Healthcare, Education & Social Services
occupations.description: Kerala = India's healthcare export powerhouse. Kozhikode, Ernakulam hospital hubs. 100% literacy; highest nurse-to-population ratio nationally.
sources: Kerala Health Systems Resource Centre Report 2023-24

occupations.name: Tourism & Hospitality (God's Own Country)
occupations.description: ₹42,000 Cr tourism revenue FY24. Backwaters, Munnar tea estates, Kovalam beach, ayurvedic wellness. International tourist arrivals fastest growing in South India.
sources: Kerala Tourism Dept. Annual Report 2023-24 — ₹42K Cr revenue
Ministry of Tourism India's top-10 states FY24

occupations.name: Agriculture, Spices & Coconut
occupations.description: Kerala produces 95% of India's black pepper, 80% of rubber, 50% of coconut. Spice Board certified organic farming growing.
sources: Spice Board of India Annual Report 2023-24 — Kerala 95% pepper
Rubber Board Annual Report 2023-24 — Kerala 80% rubber

occupations.name: IT & Digital Services (Kochi, Thiruvananthapuram)
occupations.description: Infopark Kochi, Technopark Thiruvananthapuram — India's first IT parks. 4L IT workers. Kerala IT exports ₹30,000 Cr FY24.
sources: NASSCOM State IT Report 2025 — Kerala 4L IT workers

occupations.name: Fisheries, Trade & Services
occupations.description: Kerala = largest marine fish exporter (₹8,000 Cr). Kochi = spice/commodity trade hub; Kozhikode and Thrissur trade centres.

sectors.name: Odisha
sources: Odisha Economic Survey 2023-24, Finance Dept. GoO

occupations.name: Agriculture & Tribal Forest Economy
occupations.description: Odisha = major rice, pulse, minor forest produce (MFP) state. 26.9% tribal population relies on forest economy. Agriculture 50% of workforce.
sources: OSFDC (Odisha State Forest Dev. Corp.) Tribal Economy Report 2023

occupations.name: Steel, Mining & Metals (India's Resource Hub)
occupations.description: Odisha produces 50% of India's iron ore; home to JSW Steel (Dolvi), SAIL (Rourkela), Tata Steel (Kalinganagar, world's most modern plant), NALCO aluminium.
sources: Ministry of Mines Annual Report 2023-24 — Odisha 50% iron ore

occupations.name: IT & Knowledge Economy (Bhubaneswar)
occupations.description: Infocity Bhubaneswar, Startup Odisha, C-DAC hub. Odisha IT exports growing 25%+ YoY from a low base. IIT Bhubaneswar talent pipeline.

occupations.name: Construction & Infra
occupations.description: Odisha = poster child for infrastructure governance. Major port, expressway, industrial corridor investments. GSDP growing 11%+ CAGR.

occupations.name: Trade, Handlooms & Tourism
occupations.description: Puri Jagannath tourism (1Cr+ pilgrims), Konark Sun Temple. Sambalpuri & Kotpad handlooms, Dhokra metalcraft. Odisha tourism revenues ₹10,000 Cr FY24.

sectors.name: Punjab
sources: Punjab Economic Survey 2023-24, Finance Dept. GoPb

occupations.name: Agriculture (India's Granary)
occupations.description: Punjab contributes 50% of India's central grain pool (wheat) from 1.5% of land. Farm MSP reforms and paddy-water crisis driving crop diversification.
sources: Food Corporation of India Procurement Data FY24 — Punjab 50% wheat

occupations.name: Manufacturing & Engineering (Ludhiana Hub)
occupations.description: Ludhiana = 'Manchester of India': hosiery, bicycles (Hero Cycles), sewing machines, auto parts. Punjab knitwear exports ₹10,000 Cr.
sources: PHDCCI (Punjab, Haryana Delhi Chamber of Commerce) Punjab Industry Report 2024

occupations.name: Trade, Remittance & Diaspora Economy
occupations.description: Largest Indian diaspora state (Canada, UK, US, Gulf). Remittances ₹50,000 Cr/yr. Amritsar border trade with Pakistan (limited). NRI investment in real estate.
sources: RBI State Finances Study 2023-24 — Punjab remittances

occupations.name: Transport, Trucking & Logistics
occupations.description: Punjab is India's trucking hub — connects north India to rest of country. Ludhiana dry port, Attari-Wagah land customs.

occupations.name: Government, Defence & Education
occupations.description: High military enrollment (Sikh regiments). Chandigarh UT government. Amritsar SGPC religious employment.

sectors.name: Haryana
sources: Haryana Economic Survey 2023-24, Finance Dept. GoHR

occupations.name: Automobile & Manufacturing (Gurugram/Manesar/Faridabad)
occupations.description: Maruti Suzuki Manesar (largest plant globally), Hero MotoCorp HQ, Honda Tapukara, JCB Ballabhgarh. Haryana produces 60% of India's passenger cars.
sources: SIAM Haryana Auto Production Data 2024 — 60% passenger cars
HSIIDC (Haryana State Industrial Dev.) Annual Report 2024

occupations.name: IT, Finance & Corporate HQ (Gurugram)
occupations.description: Gurugram = Corporate India's backyard: 200+ Fortune 500 companies, 50+ unicorns. Cyberabad-like IT concentration; banking, consulting, e-commerce HQs.
sources: Haryana EDB (Economic Development Board) FDI Data 2023-24

occupations.name: Agriculture (Wheat, Rice, Dairy)
occupations.description: Haryana = 2nd in per-hectare wheat yield. Karnal basmati rice, Rohtak dairy (Verka, Mother Dairy). Crop diversification push ongoing.

occupations.name: Trade, Logistics & E-commerce
occupations.description: Haryana is India's logistics gateway: Kundli-Manesar-Palwal (KMP) corridor, Tauru logistics park, Amazon/Flipkart NCR warehouses.

occupations.name: Government & Defence Establishments
occupations.description: High military enrolment (Jat and Ahir regiments). HPSC state government recruits. IAF bases at Ambala, Hisar, Sirsa.

sectors.name: Jharkhand
sources: Jharkhand Economic Survey 2023-24, Finance Dept. GoJH

occupations.name: Mining, Steel & Minerals (Dhanbad/Jamshedpur)
occupations.description: Jharkhand has 40% of India's coal, 25% of iron ore, major copper, mica. Tata Steel Jamshedpur (oldest steel plant), Bokaro Steel (SAIL), Dhanbad coalfields.
sources: Ministry of Mines Jharkhand data 2023-24 — 40% India coal reserves
Tata Steel Jamshedpur Plant Annual Report 2023-24

occupations.name: Agriculture, Forest & Tribal Economy
occupations.description: 28% tribal population (Santali, Ho, Mundari). Lac, tasar silk, kendu leaf collection. Rice, maize subsistence farming in dense forest zones.
sources: JSLPS (Jharkhand State Livelihood Promotion Society) Annual Report 2023-24

occupations.name: Construction & Infrastructure
occupations.description: Jharkhand Accelerated Road Project, Ranchi Metro, Deoghar Airport. GSDP growing at 8%+ CAGR driven by infra capex.

occupations.name: Government, Services & Education
occupations.description: JPSC state government, Central government (CIL HQ Kolkata-linked Dhanbad), NIT Jamshedpur, IIM Ranchi ecosystem.

sectors.name: Assam
sources: Assam Economic Survey 2023-24, Finance Dept. GoA

occupations.name: Tea Plantations & Agriculture
occupations.description: Assam produces 55% of India's tea; 8L permanent + 12L seasonal tea workers. Rice, jute, banana major crops. Assam tea export ₹5,000 Cr.
sources: Tea Board of India Annual Report 2023-24 — Assam 55% production

occupations.name: Oil, Gas & Petrochemicals (Digboi-Duliajan)
occupations.description: Digboi = world's oldest oil refinery (1889). OIL India (Duliajan), Numaligarh Refinery, GAIL. Brahmaputra Valley is India's oldest petroleum zone.

occupations.name: Handlooms, Silk & Crafts
occupations.description: Assam produces Muga silk (GI tag; world exclusive), Eri silk, Mekhela Chador handlooms. 15L weavers; government support through NEHHDC.
sources: CSB (Central Silk Board) Assam Muga silk data 2023-24

occupations.name: Construction & BTC Development
occupations.description: PMGSY roads, Assam Mala road project, Guwahati Metro Phase 1, Bodoland Territorial Council development. Infrastructure fastest-growing employment segment.
sources: GoA Public Works Dept. Annual Report 2023-24

occupations.name: Government, Education & Services
occupations.description: APSC state recruits, Central government (ONGC, BSNL, NF Railway), IIT Guwahati, Gauhati University. Gateway to Northeast India.

sectors.name: Delhi NCT
sources: Delhi Economic Survey 2023-24, Finance Dept. GoDL

occupations.name: Central Government & Administration
occupations.description: North Block, South Block, Ministries, UPSC, SSC, Supreme Court, Parliament, Armed Forces HQs — Delhi is India's administrative core.
sources: DoP&T Annual Report 2023-24 — Central govt HQ employment

occupations.name: IT, Startups & Digital Economy
occupations.description: Delhi NCR = largest startup ecosystem outside Bengaluru. Zomato, Paytm, PolicyBazaar, Info Edge HQ. NCR's IT-BPM workforce exceeds 10L.
sources: NASSCOM State IT Report 2025 — Delhi NCR 1M+ IT workers
Inc42 Delhi NCR Startup Ecosystem Report 2024

occupations.name: Trade, Wholesale & Retail Markets
occupations.description: Chandni Chowk, Karol Bagh, Sarojini Nagar, INA market. Delhi = India's largest wholesale trade hub for electronics, textiles, spices, hardware.
sources: Delhi Chamber of Commerce Annual Report 2024

occupations.name: Hospitality, Media & Creative
occupations.description: National media capital: Times of India, NDTV, Zee, OTT production. 1,200+ hotels; MICE capital (Pragati Maidan ITPO). G20 Summit 2023 boost.
sources: Ministry of Tourism Delhi hotel data 2023-24

occupations.name: Construction & Metro-Urban Development
occupations.description: Delhi Metro Phase 4 (65 km), redevelopment of rail colonies (DUSIB), DDA housing. NCR construction mega-projects ongoing.

occupations.name: Gig, Logistics & Personal Services
occupations.description: Delhi NCR = India's gig capital. Swiggy/Zomato/Zepto delivery, Rapido/Ola/Uber, Urban Company home services. Highest density of gig workers in India.
sources: NITI Aayog Gig Economy Report 2022 — Delhi highest density

sectors.name: Chhattisgarh
sources: Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2023-24, Finance Dept. GoCG

occupations.name: Steel, Mining & Power (India's Energy State)
occupations.description: Chhattisgarh = 3rd largest steel-producing state. Bhilai Steel Plant (SAIL, flagship). Coal reserves in Korba, Raigarh. Jindal Steel & Power, NMDC iron ore.
sources: Ministry of Mines CG mineral data 2023-24

occupations.name: Agriculture, Forest & Tribal Economy
occupations.description: CG is India's 'rice bowl' state; 33% tribal. Tendu patta, bamboo, mahua MFP collection. Bastar tribal economy + govt MSP support.

occupations.name: Power Generation & Distribution
occupations.description: CG generates 14,000 MW thermal power, supplying 8 states. NTPC Sipat, CSEB thermal plants. Power surplus state.
sources: CSERC (CG State Electricity Regulatory Commission) Annual Report 2023-24

occupations.name: Government, Trade & Services
occupations.description: CGPSC state government, Raipur trade hub (steel, agri commodities), AIIMS Raipur, IIT Bhilai ecosystem.

sectors.name: Uttarakhand & Himachal Pradesh
sources: UK Economic Survey 2023-24 + HP Economic Survey 2023-24

occupations.name: Pharma & FMCG Manufacturing (Haridwar/Baddi)
occupations.description: Haridwar/Roorkee (UK) + Baddi (HP) = India's largest pharma manufacturing belt with tax incentives. Patanjali, ITC, Hindustan Unilever plants.
sources: UK Industrial Dev. Dept. Annual Report 2023-24
HP Industrial Dev. Report 2023-24 — Baddi pharma cluster

occupations.name: Tourism, Pilgrimage & Hospitality
occupations.description: Char Dham (Kedarnath, Badrinath, Gangotri, Yamunotri), Rishikesh yoga tourism, Shimla-Manali adventure tourism. Combined 5Cr+ pilgrims/tourists FY24.

occupations.name: Horticulture, Apples & Agriculture
occupations.description: HP = India's apple capital (70% of India's apple production). UK = ginger, turmeric, Basmati rice (Dehradun). High-altitude horticulture.
sources: HP Horticulture Dept. Annual Report 2023-24 — 70% India apple

occupations.name: Hydro Power & Defence
occupations.description: Combined 30,000+ MW hydro potential in Himalayan states. NTPC Tehri, NHPC projects. Defence establishments (Lansdowne, Chakrata, Subathu).

occupations.name: Government & Education Services
occupations.description: UKPSC/HPPSC state recruits. IIT Roorkee, Doon School, Rashtriya Indian Military College anchor education economy.

sectors.name: Northeast & Smaller States
sources: DONER (Ministry of Development of NE Region) Annual Report 2023-24

occupations.name: Agriculture, Horticulture & Organic Farming
occupations.description: NE = India's organic farming frontier. Sikkim = 100% organic (first state globally). Assam tea, Nagaland spices, Meghalaya organic turmeric & ginger.

occupations.name: Tourism, Culture & Eco-Tourism
occupations.description: Kaziranga (one-horned rhino), Cherrapunji (wettest place), Tawang monastery, Hornbill Festival (Nagaland). NE tourism fastest-growing in India at 15%+ CAGR.
sources: Ministry of Tourism NE States data 2023-24

occupations.name: Government, Defence & Border Security
occupations.description: NE = high defence and CAPF presence (Assam Rifles, BSF, ITBP, CRPF). Central government the largest formal employer across all 8 NE states.
sources: MHA Annual Report 2023-24 — NE CAPF deployment

occupations.name: Handlooms, Bamboo & Forest Crafts
occupations.description: NE = world's richest bamboo forest; 66% of India's bamboo. Manipuri Moirangphee weaving, Naga shawls, Mizo puan, Arunachali cane crafts.
sources: NEHHDC (NE Handicrafts & Handlooms Dev. Corp.) Annual Report 2023-24

occupations.name: Infrastructure & Act East Policy
occupations.description: ₹76,000 Cr NE capex in FY24 under PM-DevINE, NESIDS, DONER. Roads, bridges, Jiribam-Imphal rail, Greenfield airports at Hollongi, Deoghar.
sources: BRO (Border Roads Organisation) NE projects FY24
